headline: Meta pops 8% on AI cloud monetization push
detail: Meta proving it can sell excess AI compute capacity is a meaningful positive for the whole AI infrastructure thesis - it validates the capex cycle that drives semiconductor demand. This is directly bullish for your chip-heavy book: SMH, SOXL, and names like AMAT and KLAC in your watchlist all benefit from sustained AI spending confidence. Narrow Nasdaq leadership means this kind of catalyst matters a lot to your concentrated portfolio.
rec: No immediate trade needed - you're already long SMH and SOXL which capture this. If SMH holds today's strength and AMAT pulls back to the scanner entry zone of $679.16-$679.84 with stop $661.68, that's worth a closer look - but only if chip momentum stays bid.

headline: Warsh warns not to expect Fed rate-cut hints
detail: Fed staying opaque on future moves means the market can't price in rate cuts, which keeps pressure on high-multiple tech valuations. Your book is nearly 90% tech and chip names - all of which are sensitive to rate expectations. This isn't a crash signal but it removes a tailwind.
rec: No action needed today, but if rate-sensitive pressure builds and QQQ starts threatening the $700 area, revisit your margin sizing - your QQQ stop is $670 and that's a real drawdown from here.

headline: Almost all Nasdaq-100 gains in H1 2026 came from just 10 stocks
detail: This confirms the rally is dangerously narrow - if you're in those 10 names you've done well, but concentration risk is real. For a margin trader with 90% tech exposure, a rotation or unwind in those leaders hits the whole book simultaneously with no offset. This is a structural risk reminder, not an immediate sell signal.
rec: No trade today, but this is a nudge to make sure your stops on QQQ ($670) and SPY ($705) are actually set - they're your hedges against a broad unwind and you should treat them seriously.

headline: ServiceNow and Salesforce look like buys as Armageddon fears too extreme
detail: An analyst calling CRM and NOW oversold on extreme pessimism is a contrarian signal in enterprise software. CRM appears on your scanner as a grade-C WATCH setup with entry $158.46-$158.66 and stop $155.97. Grade C means weak trend alignment - daily is down, 4H is flat.
rec: CRM scanner entry $158.46-$158.66, stop $155.97 is technically there, but grade C with a daily downtrend is a low-conviction setup - only consider a very small position given your margin exposure, and only if price actually holds that entry zone today.
